name: Brihadeeswarar Temple
stories: Built in the 11th century as a symbol of Chola dynasty power.
The temple tower rises to 66 meters, one of the tallest in the world.
UNESCO World Heritage site, part of the Great Living Chola Temples.
Known for its massive granite structures and intricate frescoes.
The temple was constructed entirely of granite, despite no quarries nearby.

name: Jagannath Temple
stories: One of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites.
Famous for the annual Rath Yatra festival.
The temple kitchen is considered the largest in the world, feeding thousands daily.
Legends say the idols are replaced every 12 years in a sacred ritual.
The temple’s flag is changed every day, symbolizing divine protection.

name: Kedarnath Temple
stories: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, part of Char Dham Yatra.
Survived multiple floods and natural disasters.
Believed to be built near the site where Pandavas sought forgiveness.
The temple is surrounded by snow-clad peaks, adding to its mystique.
Pilgrims believe visiting Kedarnath cleanses sins and grants liberation.

name: Konark Sun Temple
stories: Temple shaped like a giant chariot with 24 wheels.
Dedicated to the Sun God, Surya.
Intricate carvings depict daily life, animals, and celestial beings.
Legends say the temple once had a massive lodestone that disrupted ships' compasses.
The wheels of the temple also function as sundials, showing time accurately.

name: Somnath Temple
stories: One of the 12 Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva.
Destroyed and rebuilt over 17 times in history.
Visited by Mahmud of Ghazni, who looted its treasures.
Modern reconstruction was completed in 1951 under Sardar Patel.
The temple is believed to have been originally built by the Moon God.

name: Akshardham Temple
stories: Modern Hindu temple showcasing Indian culture and spirituality.
Features a massive central monument carved from pink sandstone.
Includes exhibitions on Indian history, values, and traditions.
The musical fountain depicts the cycle of life and death.
The temple complex also houses gardens symbolizing peace and harmony.

name: Angkor Wat
stories: Largest religious monument in the world.
Originally Hindu, later converted to Buddhist worship.
Its bas-reliefs depict epic stories from Ramayana and Mahabharata.
The temple is aligned with celestial events like equinoxes.
Angkor Wat symbolizes Mount Meru, the center of the universe in Hindu cosmology.

name: Machu Picchu
stories: Inca citadel rediscovered in 1911 by Hiram Bingham.
Believed to be a royal estate or sacred religious site.
Its terraces showcase advanced Inca agricultural techniques.
UNESCO World Heritage site and one of the New Seven Wonders.
The site was built without mortar, yet stones fit perfectly together.

name: Petra
stories: City carved into rose-red cliffs.
Known as the 'Lost City' rediscovered in 1812.
The Treasury (Al-Khazneh) is its most iconic structure.
Petra was a major trading hub connecting Arabia, Egypt, and Syria.
The city had advanced water management systems with aqueducts and cisterns.

name: Borobudur Temple
stories: Largest Buddhist monument in the world.
Built as a mandala representing the Buddhist cosmos.
Contains over 500 Buddha statues and 2,600 relief panels.
Abandoned after volcanic eruptions, rediscovered in the 19th century.
The temple is designed as a step pyramid symbolizing the path to enlightenment.
